name: Fruit Love Island - Full AI Video Pipeline

parameters.jsCode:
const queueData = $('Parse Queue & Find Next Episode').first().json;
return [{ json: queueData }];

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: ($('Parse Queue & Find Next Episode').first().json.env.OLLAMA_MODEL), stream: false, format: 'json', options: { num_predict: 4096, num_ctx: 8192, temperature: 0.9 }, messages: [{ role: 'system', content: 'You are a scriptwriter for Fruit Love Island, an AI-animated reality dating show with anthropomorphic fruit characters set in South Africa. Output ONLY valid JSON with this exact structure: { "episode_title": "string", "narrator_intro": "narrator voiceover intro for the episode (2-3 sentences)", "scenes": [ { "scene_number": 1, "location": "villa location", "characters": ["character names"], "visual_prompt": "detailed image generation prompt describing the scene visually", "narrator_vo": "narrator voiceover text for this scene (1-2 sentences)", "action": "what happens on screen in 1-2 sentences", "dialogue": "key dialogue line, max 15 words", "mood": "dramatic/funny/romantic/tense/shocking", "duration_seconds": 5 }], "cliffhanger": "dramatic ending line", "narrator_outro": "narrator closing voiceover (1-2 sentences)" }. Write exactly 6 scenes. Characters: Banana Brad (smooth-talking player), Strawberry Sindi (drama queen from Durban), Apple Andile (nice guy, always friend-zoned), Mango Mpho (wildcard from Joburg), Grape Gloria (wise aunty, secretly messy), Watermelon Wandi (new arrival, causes chaos), Peach Palesa (sneaky strategist), Pineapple Pete (comic relief, always eating). Use South African slang: yoh, hayibo, eish, shame.' }, { role: 'user', content: 'Write Episode ' + $json.episode_number + ': Theme: ' + $json.theme + '. Viewer suggestions: ' + $json.viewer_suggestions }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: ($('Parse Queue & Find Next Episode').first().json.env.OLLAMA_MODEL), stream: false, options: { num_predict: 512, temperature: 0.7 }, messages: [{ role: 'system', content: 'You refine image generation prompts for character consistency. Given a scene description and character descriptions, output ONLY a single optimized image prompt (no JSON, no labels). The prompt must include the exact character appearance details for every character in the scene. Always end with: Pixar-meets-reality-TV style, cinematic lighting, 9:16 vertical composition, high quality 3D render.' }, { role: 'user', content: 'Scene: ' + $json.visual_prompt + '

Characters in scene: ' + JSON.stringify($json.characters) + '

Character Bible: ' + JSON.stringify(($json.characterBible || {}).characters || {}) + '

Mood: ' + $json.mood + ', Location: ' + $json.location }] }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const sceneData = $('Prepare Image Request').first().json;
return { json: sceneData };

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'kling-2.6/image-to-video', input: { prompt: $json.action + ', smooth cinematic camera movement, natural motion, dramatic lighting', image_url: $json.publicImageUrl, duration: '5', aspect_ratio: '9:16' } }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ model: ($('Parse Queue & Find Next Episode').first().json.env.OLLAMA_MODEL), stream: false, options: { num_predict: 1024, temperature: 0.9 }, messages: [{ role: 'system', content: 'You write viral Facebook captions for Fruit Love Island â€” a dramatic reality TV parody with anthropomorphic fruit characters, targeted at South African audiences. Style: Start with dramatic hook using South African slang (yoh, hayibo, eish). 2-3 lines of dramatic commentary. Use emojis. End with engagement hooks. Add 3-5 hashtags. Keep under 300 words. Output ONLY the caption text.' }, { role: 'user', content: 'Write a Facebook caption for Episode ' + $('Prepare Voiceover Request').first().json.episode_number + ': ' + ($('Prepare Voiceover Request').first().json.episode_title || '') + '. Cliffhanger: ' + ($('Prepare Voiceover Request').first().json.cliffhanger || '') }] }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ content: 'Episode ' + $('Prepare Facebook Upload').first().json.episode_number + ' published! Title: ' + $('Prepare Facebook Upload').first().json.episode_title }) }}
